title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
description: Given the heads of two singly linked lists, headA and headB, return the node where the two lists intersect. If the two linked lists have no intersection at all, return null. For simplicity, you may assume that there are no cycles in the linked lists. Note that your solution should be O(n) time complexity and O(1) space complexity.

examples.explanation: The linked lists intersect at the node with value 8.
The intersection occurs at the node with value 2.
There is no intersection between the two linked lists.

constraints: The number of nodes in both linked lists is in the range [0, 10^4].
The values of the nodes are within the range [-10^4, 10^4].
hints: Consider using the two-pointer technique to traverse both lists.
If one pointer reaches the end of a list, redirect it to the beginning of the other list.
Keep track of the length of both linked lists to handle different lengths.
Check for intersections after aligning the pointers at the same distance from the end.
